steps.title: Substitute the value x = 2
steps.content: Replace $x$ with $2$ in $$f(x) = x^3 + 9.$$ This gives $$f(2) = (2)^3 + 9.$$

steps.title: Compute the result
steps.content: Simplify: $$f(2) = 8 + 9 = 17.$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice C is correct. It's given that $f\left(x\right)={x}^{3}+9$. Substituting $2$ for $x$ in this equation yields $f\left(2\right)={\left(2\right)}^{3}+9$. This is equivalent to $f\left(2\right)=8+9$, or $f\left(2\right)=17$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A is incorrect. This is the value of $2+3+9$, not ${2}^{3}+9$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect. This is the value of $2\left(3\right)+9$, not ${2}^{3}+9$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice D is incorrect. This is the value of ${3}^{2}+9$, not ${2}^{3}+9$.
